input.background: A study compares the outcomes of a treatment group and a control group. In the treatment group, 40 out of 320 individuals (12.5%) experience an outcome, while in the control group, 26 out of 336 individuals (7.7%) experience the outcome. The data is presented in a crosstabulated form (Table 18.28). The task involves calculating chi-square statistics and P-values using both Pearson's and continuity-corrected (Yates') chi-square methods and interpreting the results.
input.question: Calculate the chi-square statistic and P-value using Pearson's chi-square method.
